result:
Build is green. No temporary instrumentation was added.

## Report

**Root cause:** `getnum` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1654` uses `(usize::MAX as i32 - 9) / 10` as the digit-accumulation threshold. On 64-bit, `usize::MAX as i32` truncates/wraps to `-1`, so the threshold becomes `-1`, and the loop breaks after consuming the very first digit. That made `"i16"` parse as `i` with width `1`, leaving `6` to be misread as the next format option — hence the `invalid format option '6'` error on the first multi-digit pack format in tpack.lua (`packsize("!xXi16")`).

**Fix:** Replace `(usize::MAX as i32 - 9) / 10` with `(i32::MAX - 9) / 10`. The C source defines `MAXSIZE` as `(size_t)INT_MAX` on platforms where `sizeof(size_t) > sizeof(int)` (i.e. all our targets), so the correct upper bound in `i32` arithmetic is `i32::MAX`, not `usize::MAX` cast down.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Per instructions I did not re-run the test program; the mega-loop will re-test. The fix should let `packsize("!xXi16")`, `packsize("i16")`, and every other multi-digit width in tpack.lua parse correctly.
